---
title: "Installation"
toc: true
---

You can install `repos` as a system package (Ubuntu/Debian, macOS, Windows), from
source, or as a language-specific wrapper (R or Python).

## Ubuntu / Debian

### Option 1: Install from APT Repository (Recommended)

Install and keep `repos` up to date directly via `apt`:

```bash
# Add repository signing key
curl -fsSL https://miguelrodo.github.io/apt-miguelrodo/KEY.gpg \
   | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/apt-miguelrodo.gpg

# Add apt source
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/apt-miguelrodo.gpg] https://miguelrodo.github.io/apt-miguelrodo stable main" \
   | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/apt-miguelrodo.list >/dev/null

# Install repos
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y repos
```

To uninstall:

```bash
sudo apt-get remove repos
```

### Option 2: Local Installation (No sudo required)

Install the latest precompiled `repos` binary into the first writable directory
already present in your `PATH`:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
cd repos
bash install-local.sh
```

If no writable path entry exists, the installer will stop with guidance.

To uninstall:

```bash
bash uninstall-local.sh
```

### Option 3: System-wide from Release `.deb` (requires sudo)

Prefer Option 1 (APT) for automatic updates. If needed, you can manually download
and install a release `.deb` from the
[Releases page](https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos/releases):

```bash
VERSION_REPOS=1.2.6
ARCH="$(dpkg --print-architecture)"
wget "https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos/releases/download/v${VERSION_REPOS}/repos_${VERSION_REPOS}_${ARCH}.deb"
sudo dpkg -i "repos_${VERSION_REPOS}_${ARCH}.deb"
rm "repos_${VERSION_REPOS}_${ARCH}.deb"

# Fix any dependency issues
sudo apt-get install -f
```

To uninstall:

```bash
sudo dpkg -r repos
```

### Dependencies

All Ubuntu/Debian methods require `bash`, `git`, `curl`, and `jq`.  These are
typically pre-installed.  If not:

```bash
sudo apt-get install bash git curl jq
```

---

## macOS (Homebrew)

```bash
brew tap MiguelRodo/repos
brew install repos
```

The formula automatically handles the `jq` dependency. Git is typically
pre-installed on macOS.

---

## Windows

### Option 1: Release Binary (Recommended)

Download and install the pre-compiled `repos.exe` into the first writable directory
already present in your `PATH`:

1. Clone the repository:
   ```powershell
   git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
   cd repos
   ```
2. Run the installer:
   ```powershell
   .\install.ps1
   ```
3. The installer places `repos.exe` in the first writable directory already in your
   current `PATH`.

**Requirements:** PowerShell and network access to GitHub releases.

### Option 2: Scoop

```powershell
scoop bucket add repos https://github.com/MiguelRodo/scoop-bucket
scoop install repos
```

`git` and `jq` are installed automatically by Scoop. You will also need
[Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win) for Bash support.

### Manual

1. Clone the repository:
   ```powershell
   git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
   cd repos
   ```
2. Run the installer:
   ```powershell
   .\install.ps1
   ```
3. The installer places `repos.exe` in the first writable directory already in your
   current `PATH`.

**Requirements:** PowerShell (with `Invoke-WebRequest`) and network access to GitHub releases.

---

## From Source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
cd repos

# Local install (no sudo)
bash install-local.sh

# Or system-wide on Ubuntu/Debian
sudo dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -b
sudo dpkg -i ../repos_*.deb
```

---

## Language Wrappers

### R Package

```r
install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("MiguelRodo/repos")
```

Requires `bash`, `git`, `curl`, and `jq` on your system.

### Python Package

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
```

Or from a local clone:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
cd repos
pip install .
```

Requires `bash`, `git`, `curl`, and `jq` on your system.  On Windows, use
[Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win) or WSL.

---

## Verify Installation

```bash
repos --help
```
